figures.caption: Table 2: In-domain results on Qwen3-4B and Qwen3-8B. All methods are trained to convergence, and scores are reported as the maximum mean@16(%) achieved during training. Boldface indicates the best result, and underline indicates the second-best result.
Figure 6: Training efficiency. We report time(seconds) per step for total steps and last 50 steps on Chemistry with Qwen3-8B.
Figure 7: Response length dynamics. A rolling average of response length over 10 steps on Chemistry with Qwen3-8B.

Figure 1: Left: Pilot study. We use SDPO (Hübotter et al., 2026) as a representative OPSD method. All methods are trained on the Material dataset with Qwen3-8B, and evaluated on Material and ToolUse as the in-domain and out-of-domain benchmarks, respectively. Right: Comparison of post-training methods. Green and red tokens denote the valid prefix before the first error and the error-affected suffix, respectively. (a) RLVR provides only outcome-level feedback for a sampled rollout. (b) Standard OPSD conditions the self-teacher on a correct solution, yielding dense token-level supervision but distilling the entire response. (c) ROSD first uses a reflector to extract a corrective idea and an error quote; the corrective idea guides the self-teacher, while the error quote localizes updates to the error-affected suffix.

Table 3: Out-of-domain results. All methods are trained to convergence on the training dataset and scores are reported as mean@16 (%) averaged over the last three evaluation steps. 4B and 8B denote Qwen3-4B and Qwen38B, respectively. Boldface indicates the best result, and underline indicates the second-best result.
Figure 3: In domain training dynamics on Material and Chemistry. We report the mean@16 test score, and the shaded regions indicate the variance over 16 sampled responses.

Table 4: Ablation study on Qwen3-4B and Qwen3-8B. Scores are reported as mean@16 (%). 4B and 8B denote Qwen3-4B and Qwen3-8B, respectively. Boldface indicates the best result, and underline indicates the second-best result. For in-domain results, we report the maximum value achieved during training. For OOD results, each dataset name denotes the training dataset, and each score reports the average last 3 steps performance over the remaining out-of-domain datasets.

Figure 2: Self-reflection prompt templates used by ROSD. The system prompt provides task-level instructions to the reflector. In the prompt templates, {problem} is replaced with the problem x; {correct_rollout} is replaced with the selected correct rollout, i.e., y∗ in Equation 5 and y+ in Equation 6; {wrong_rollout} is replaced with the wrong rollout y−; and {answer} is replaced with the correct final answer. The outputs enclosed by <error_quote> and <explanation> correspond to the error quote q and the key corrective idea e, respectively.
